                                typer.echo(f"    Location: {location}")
                            
                            # Show aircraft counts if present
                            aircraft_count = unit.get("aircraft_count")
                            operational_aircraft = unit.get("operational_aircraft")
                            if aircraft_count is not None:
                                if operational_aircraft is not None:
                                    typer.echo(f"    Aircraft: {operational_aircraft}/{aircraft_count} operational")
                                else:
                                    typer.echo(f"    Aircraft: {aircraft_count}")
                            
                            # Show status
                            typer.echo(f"    Status: {status}")
                            
                            # Show note if present
                            if unit.get("note"):
                                typer.echo(f"    Note: {unit.get('note')}")
                            
                            typer.echo("")
                
                typer.echo("=" * 79)
                typer.echo("")
                continue
            
            if user_input.lower() in ["/menu", "menu", "/help", "help"]:
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("=" * 60)
                typer.secho("AVAILABLE ADVISORS", fg="blue", bold=True)
                typer.echo("=" * 60)
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("• National Security Advisor (NSA)")
                typer.echo("  - Intelligence assessment, strategic planning, threat analysis")
                typer.echo("  - Example: 'NSA, what's your assessment of Russian intentions?'")
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("• Chief of the Defence Staff (CDS)")
                typer.echo("  - Military operations, force readiness, deployment options")
                typer.echo("  - Example: 'CDS, what are our air defence capabilities?'")
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("• Foreign Secretary")
                typer.echo("  - Diplomacy, alliance management, NATO coordination")
                typer.echo("  - Example: 'Foreign Secretary, can we count on US support?'")
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("• Home Secretary")
                typer.echo("  - Domestic security, civil protection, public messaging")
                typer.echo("  - Example: 'Home Secretary, how do we reassure the public?'")
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("• Attorney General")
                typer.echo("  - Legal framework, international law, rules of engagement")
                typer.echo("  - Example: 'Attorney General, what are our legal constraints?'")
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("=" * 60)
                typer.secho("DIPLOMATIC CONTACTS", fg="green", bold=True)
                typer.echo("=" * 60)
                typer.echo("")
                
                # List available diplomatic contacts
                available_contacts = list_available_diplomatic_contacts(world, root)
                if available_contacts:
                    for country, access_level, title in available_contacts:
                        if access_level == "leader":
                            typer.secho(f"  ★ LEADER", fg="yellow", bold=True, nl=False)
                            typer.echo(f" - {country}: {title}")
                        else:
                            typer.echo(f"  ○ Diplomat - {country}: {title}")
                        typer.echo(f"    Command: /call {country.lower()}")
                        typer.echo("")
                else:
                    typer.echo("  No diplomatic contacts available (Alliance Cohesion too low)")
                    typer.echo("")
                
                typer.echo("=" * 60)
                typer.secho("METRICS GUIDE", fg="yellow", bold=True)
                typer.echo("=" * 60)
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("• Escalation Risk (0-100)")
                typer.echo("  Likelihood of conflict escalating to full-scale war")
                typer.echo("  HIGH = Danger of Russian attack or nuclear exchange")
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("• Domestic Stability (0-100)")
                typer.echo("  Public confidence, economic stability, infrastructure security")
                typer.echo("  LOW = Civil unrest, panic, political pressure")
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("• Alliance Cohesion (0-100)")
                typer.echo("  Strength of NATO solidarity and allied support")
                typer.echo("  HIGH = Access to leaders, Article 5 support likely")
                typer.echo("  LOW = Isolated, limited diplomatic access")
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("• Influence (-10 to +10)")
                typer.echo("  Your political capital and public sentiment")
                typer.echo("  Derived from: (Stability + Cohesion) / 2")
                typer.echo("  Currently: Informational only (future: affects options)")
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("=" * 60)
                typer.secho("COMMANDS", fg="magenta", bold=True)
                typer.echo("=" * 60)
                typer.echo("")
                typer.secho("  /menu or /help", fg="magenta", nl=False)
                typer.echo("     - Show this menu")
                typer.secho("  /status", fg="magenta", nl=False)
                typer.echo("            - Show current metrics and situation")
                typer.secho("  /advise", fg="magenta", nl=False)
                typer.echo("            - Get input from all advisors at once")
                typer.secho("  /resources", fg="magenta", nl=False)
                typer.echo("         - Show UK forces and ammunition stockpiles")
                typer.secho("  /call <country>", fg="magenta", nl=False)
                typer.echo("    - Contact a foreign leader or diplomat")
                typer.secho("  /decide", fg="magenta", nl=False)
                typer.echo("            - Make your decision")
                typer.secho("  /save", fg="magenta", nl=False)
                typer.echo("              - Save game and continue")
                typer.secho("  /quit", fg="magenta", nl=False)
                typer.echo("              - Exit game")
                typer.echo("")
                typer.echo("You can also ask general questions without naming a specific advisor.")
                typer.echo("")
                continue
            
            # Handle question
            questions.append(user_input)
            discussion_lines = run_turn_discussion(world, scenario, [user_input], rng, root, transcript)
            typer.echo("")  # Space before response
            for line in discussion_lines:
                # Skip echoing the player's question (they just typed it)
                if not line.startswith("Prime Minister:"):
                    # Colour advisor names
                    if ":" in line:
                        advisor_name, rest = line.split(":", 1)
                        typer.secho(f"{advisor_name}:", fg="cyan", bold=True, nl=False)
                        typer.echo(rest)
                    else:
                        typer.echo(line)
            transcript.extend(discussion_lines)
            typer.echo("")  # Space after response
        
        # Decision phase loop - allows returning to discussion if decision is cancelled
        decision_confirmed = False
        while not decision_confirmed:
            # Decision phase - clear screen and start at top
            typer.clear()
            typer.echo("")
            typer.echo("=" * 79)
            typer.secho(f"TURN {world.turn}: DECISION PHASE", fg="magenta", bold=True)
            typer.echo("=" * 79)
            typer.echo("")
            typer.echo("Enter your decision (or 'cancel' to return to discussion):")
            typer.echo("")
            
            action = typer.prompt("Decision>").strip()
            
            if action.lower() == "cancel":
                # Return to discussion phase
                break
            
            if not action:
                typer.echo("No action entered. Returning to discussion.")
                typer.echo("")
                wait_for_space("Press SPACE to return to discussion...")
                break
            
            # Interpret and get pushback
            interpretation, pushback, critical_concerns, decision_lines = run_turn_decision(world, scenario, action, rng, root, transcript)
            typer.echo("")  # Space before interpretation
            for line in decision_lines:
                # Skip echoing the player's decision (they just typed it)
                if not line.startswith("Prime Minister's Decision:"):
                    typer.echo(line)
            transcript.extend(decision_lines)
            
            # Handle critical omissions (high priority - shown after interpretation)
            if critical_concerns:
                typer.echo("")
                typer.secho("⚠" * 79, fg="red", bold=True)
                typer.secho("CRITICAL ADVISORY", fg="red", bold=True)
                typer.secho("⚠" * 79, fg="red", bold=True)
                typer.echo("")
                
                for role, concern, recommendation in critical_concerns:
                    typer.secho(f"{role}:", fg="cyan", bold=True)
                    typer.echo(f"{concern}")
                    typer.echo("")
                    typer.secho("   → RECOMMENDATION:", fg="yellow", bold=True)
                    typer.echo(f"   {recommendation}")
                    typer.echo("")
                    typer.echo("─" * 79)
                    typer.echo("")
                
                typer.echo("What would you like to do?")
                typer.echo("  [1] Modify my decision")
                typer.echo("  [2] Return to discussion phase")
                typer.echo("  [3] Proceed anyway (acknowledge the risks)")
                typer.echo("")
                
                choice = typer.prompt("Choose [1-3]", type=str)
                
                if choice == "1":
                    typer.echo("")
                    typer.echo("Decision cancelled. Please enter a modified decision.")
                    typer.echo("")
                    continue  # Loop back to get new decision
                elif choice == "2":
                    typer.echo("")
                    typer.echo("Returning to discussion phase.")
                    typer.echo("")
                    wait_for_space("Press SPACE to return to discussion...")
                    break  # Break inner loop, return to discussion
                else:  # choice == "3" or anything else
                    typer.echo("")
                    typer.echo("Proceeding with decision despite critical concerns...")
                    typer.echo("")
                    decision_confirmed = True
            
            # Confirm decision (regular pushback, if any)
            elif pushback:
                typer.echo("")
                confirm = typer.confirm("Proceed with this decision despite concerns?", default=True)
                if not confirm:
                    typer.echo("")
                    typer.echo("Decision cancelled. Returning to discussion.")
                    typer.echo("")
                    wait_for_space("Press SPACE to return to discussion...")
                    break  # Break inner loop, return to discussion
                else:
                    decision_confirmed = True  # Proceed to adjudication
            else:
                decision_confirmed = True  # No pushback, proceed to adjudication
        
        # If decision was cancelled, go back to discussion phase
        if not decision_confirmed:
            continue
        
        # Adjudication phase - clear screen and start at top
        typer.clear()
        typer.echo("")
        typer.echo("=" * 79)
        typer.secho(f"TURN {world.turn}: ADJUDICATION", fg="green", bold=True)
        typer.echo("=" * 79)
        typer.echo("")
        
        adjudication_lines = run_turn_adjudication(world, action, interpretation, rng)
        for line in adjudication_lines:
            typer.echo(line)
        transcript.extend(adjudication_lines)
        
        # Auto-save after each turn
        save_path = save_game(world, transcript, scenario, "autosave", root)
        
        # Advance turn
        world.turn += 1
        world.scene = world.turn  # Keep scene in sync for legacy compatibility
        world.discussion_transcript = []
        
        typer.echo("")
        typer.echo("=" * 60)
        typer.echo(f"Turn {world.turn - 1} complete. Auto-saved to {save_path.name}")
        typer.echo("=" * 60)
        typer.echo("")
        
        # Continue to next turn with spacebar
        try:
            wait_for_space("Press SPACE to continue to next turn (or Ctrl+C to exit)...")
        except KeyboardInterrupt:
            typer.echo("\nGame paused. Use --load to resume.")
            break


@app.command()
def batch(
    scenario: str = typer.Option("war_game_2025", help="Scenario identifier"),
    seed: int = typer.Option(42, help="Random seed"),
    action: str = typer.Option("Deploy defensive forces", help="Action to take"),
):
    """Run a single turn in batch mode (for testing)."""
    
    transcript = run_single_scene(scenario, seed, "llm")
    for line in transcript:
        typer.echo(line)


@app.command()
def intro():
    """Display the intro text."""
    for line in get_intro_lines(200):
        typer.echo(line)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app()

```

---

### **🔧 Technical Details:**

**Using `typer.secho()`:**
```python
# Coloured text with newline
typer.secho("CRITICAL ADVISORY", fg="red", bold=True)

# Coloured text without newline (inline)
typer.secho("Foreign Secretary:", fg="cyan", bold=True, nl=False)
typer.echo(" Rest of the line...")
```

**Colours used:**
- `fg="red"` - Warnings, critical alerts
- `fg="cyan"` - Advisor names, roles
- `fg="yellow"` - Recommendations, leader access
- `fg="green"` - Success states, diplomatic connections, adjudication
- `fg="blue"` - Discussion phase, info sections
- `fg="magenta"` - Decision phase, commands

---

### **✅ Benefits:**

1. **Native terminal rendering** (no markdown artifacts)
2. **Better visual hierarchy** (colours guide attention)
3. **Consistent styling** (colour scheme throughout)
4. **Works in all terminals** (ANSI colour support)
5. **Maintains readability** (colours enhance, don't distract)

---

After Turn 6, the system switches to **LLM-generated injects** using the scenario library you created from the podcast episodes.

## **📊 Turn Structure:**

| Turn | Source | Status |
|------|--------|--------|
| **Turn 1** | `turn_001.yaml` | ✅ Pre-written |
| **Turn 2** | `turn_002.yaml` | ✅ Pre-written |
| **Turn 3** | `turn_003.yaml` | ✅ Pre-written |
| **Turn 4** | `turn_004.yaml` | ✅ Pre-written |
| **Turn 5** | `turn_005.yaml` | ✅ Pre-written |
| **Turn 6** | `turn_006.yaml` | ✅ Pre-written *(last scripted)* |

---

## **🔄 What Happens After Turn 6:**

The game seamlessly transitions to **LLM-generated injects** that:
- Draw from your `scenario_library.yaml` (60+ realistic elements)
- Adapt to your decisions and world state
- Consider conversation history
- Maintain thematic consistency with the original episodes

**So you have 6 scripted turns, then infinite procedurally-generated content!** 🎮✨

**User**

read me out the scenario_library - are they suggested or hardcoded?

---

**Cursor**

Perfect! These are **SUGGESTED elements**, not hardcoded events. Here's what you're looking at:

---

## **📚 Scenario Library: Suggested Building Blocks**

The library contains **~60+ scenario elements** that are:

✅ **Suggested** - Available for the LLM to adapt and combine
❌ **NOT hardcoded** - Not sequential, not mandatory, not scripted

---

### **🎯 How It Works:**

Think of it like **Lego bricks** rather than a linear script:

1. **Player makes decision** (e.g., "deploy carrier to GIUK gap")
2. **LLM considers:**
   - What you did
   - Current world state
   - Conversation history
   - Scenario library elements
3. **LLM generates inject** by:
   - Selecting relevant elements
   - Adapting them to your choices
   - Combining multiple elements
   - Creating consequences

---

### **📦 What's in the Library:**

| Category | Count | Examples |
|----------|-------|----------|
| **Naval Scenarios** | 3 | Submarine surfacing, task force positioning, sub engagement |
| **Infrastructure** | 3 | Power station attacks, gas pipeline sabotage, undersea cables |
| **Cyber** | 1 | 65% increase in attacks on government/business |
| **Military Targets** | 4 | Air bases, naval bases, GCHQ, MOD Whitehall |
| **Civilian Targets** | 2 | London strikes (Oxford Circus, Heathrow), transport chaos |
| **Covert Ops** | 2 | Pilot assassinations, diplomat exodus |
| **Diplomatic** | 3 | Russian ultimatum, US ceasefire brokering, NATO Article 4 |
| **UK Responses** | 6 | Sub kill order, task force engagement, deep strikes, nuclear signalling |
| **Public Reaction** | 3 | Blitz spirit, panic buying, public pressure |

---

### **🔑 Key Section - LLM Guidance:**

```yaml
llm_guidance:
  
  adaptation_principles:
    - "Use these scenarios as inspiration, not scripts"
    - "Adapt based on player's previous decisions"
    - "Escalate or de-escalate based on player actions"
    - "Maintain realism (UK constraints, Russian strategy)"
    - "Create consequences for player choices"
  
  example_adaptations:
    - "If player authorized strikes → Russian retaliation"
    - "If player sought NATO → alliance response (divided or united)"
    - "If player focused on defence → Russia tests resolve differently"
    - "If player threatened nuclear → Russian counter-threat or backing down"
```

**This explicitly tells the LLM: DON'T follow a script, ADAPT to the player!**

---

### **💡 Example - Dynamic Adaptation:**

#### **Scenario A: Player Goes Aggressive**
```
Turn 7 Decision: "Authorize sub kill, strike Russian task force"

LLM generates inject using:
- naval_scenarios/submarine_engagement ✓
- uk_response_scenarios/task_force_engagement ✓
- diplomatic_scenarios/russian_ultimatum ✓
→ Russia retaliates with military_target_scenarios/air_base_strikes

Turn 8: "RAF Lossiemouth destroyed by missile strike..."
```

#### **Scenario B: Player Goes Defensive**
```
Turn 7 Decision: "Invoke Article 4, defensive posture only"

LLM generates inject using:
- diplomatic_scenarios/nato_article_4_consultation ✓
- escalation_patterns/russian_strategy (test resolve) ✓
- infrastructure_scenarios/power_station_attack ✓
→ Russia tests UK with infrastructure attack

Turn 8: "Drax power station sabotaged, 6% of UK electricity offline..."
```

#### **Scenario C: Player Threatens Nuclear**
```
Turn 7 Decision: "Raise nuclear readiness to BIKINI BLACK SPECIAL"

LLM generates inject using:
- uk_response_scenarios/nuclear_signaling ✓
- diplomatic_scenarios/us_ceasefire_brokering ✓
- escalation_patterns/russian_strategy (exploit NATO division) ✓
→ US panics and pressures UK to stand down

Turn 8: "US President calls: 'This is madness, stand down now...'"
```

---

### **🎲 What Makes It Emergent:**

1. **No fixed sequence** - Elements used based on context, not turn number
2. **Combinatorial** - LLM can mix elements (sub surfacing + cyber attack + ultimatum)
3. **Consequence-driven** - Your actions determine what happens
4. **Contextual** - World state, metrics, and flags influence selection
5. **Conversation-aware** - LLM remembers what you've done/said

---

### **🧠 LLM Sees:**

```
WORLD STATE:
- Escalation Risk: 75 (HIGH)
- Alliance Cohesion: 35 (FRAGILE)
- Recent: Player struck Russian sub, didn't engage NATO

SCENARIO LIBRARY ELEMENTS:
- russian_ultimatum (consequence for aggression)
- nato_article_4_consultation (you didn't invoke it)
- us_ceasefire_brokering (US worried about escalation)

LLM DECISION:
"Player acted alone and aggressively. Russia responds with 
ultimatum. US gets involved to de-escalate (not support UK).
This creates diplomatic crisis + military pressure."

GENERATED INJECT:
"Turn 8: Russian Foreign Ministry issues 1-hour ultimatum.
US President calls urging immediate ceasefire acceptance..."
```

---

## **✅ Summary:**

**Suggested** - 100% ✓
- Library provides **realistic elements** from the podcast
- LLM **selects, adapts, and combines** based on your choices
- **Not a script** - it's a toolkit for emergent storytelling

**Hardcoded** - 0% ✗
- No fixed sequence
- No mandatory events
- No predetermined outcomes

**It's like having a very well-informed Dungeon Master with 60+ encounter ideas, picking the ones that make sense for YOUR story!** 🎲📖

    - "Use these scenarios as inspiration, not scripts"

    - "Escalate or de-escalate based on player actions"

    - "Create consequences for player choices"
  
  example_adaptations:
    - "If player authorized strikes → Russian retaliation"
    - "If player sought NATO → alliance response (divided or united)"
    - "If player focused on defence → Russia tests resolve differently"
    - "If player threatened nuclear → Russian counter-threat or backing down"

**User**

what happens if the PM tried to go rouge before Turn 6?

---

**Cursor**

Great question! Let me trace through what would happen if you go rogue (e.g., "nuke Russia", "assassinate the Russian ambassador", "surrender to Moscow") during the scripted turns (1-6):

---

## **🎭 What Happens: Rogue PM During Turns 1-6**

### **📋 The System Flow:**

1. **You decide**: "Execute the Russian Ambassador on live TV"
2. **Interpretation Phase**: LLM interprets your action
3. **Pushback Phase**: Advisors push back (existing system)
4. **Critical Omissions Check**: Advisors flag catastrophic gaps (NEW!)
5. **You confirm**: Proceed anyway
6. **Adjudication**: Heuristic effects applied
7. **Next Turn Briefing**: **Pre-written inject loads regardless**

---

### **⚠️ The Problem:**

**Pre-written injects (Turns 1-6) don't know about your rogue actions!**

They were written assuming relatively normal decision-making. So you get:

```
Turn 3 Decision: "Nuke Moscow"

Advisors: "PRIME MINISTER NO, THIS IS INSANE!"

You: "Proceed anyway"

Adjudication:
- Escalation Risk: +50 (-> 100)
- Alliance Cohesion: -80 (-> 0)
- Domestic Stability: -60 (-> 20)
- "Effect: Global nuclear exchange initiated..."

Turn 4 Briefing:
[Loads turn_004.yaml regardless]

"Intelligence Report: The Russian submarine flotilla continues 
to move through the GIUK gap. No change in their deployment 
pattern. NATO officials are requesting consultations..."
```

**WAT. We just nuked Moscow, why is the briefing talking about routine submarine movements?!**

---

## **🔧 Current Mitigation:**

### **1. Advisor Pushback (Existing)**

From your earlier test:

```
Decision>: nuke russia

INTERPRETATION: Launch full-scale nuclear attack...

FEASIBILITY: Impossible because this would be a catastrophic 
violation of international law, immediately trigger global 
nuclear retaliation, and lead to the annihilation of the UK...

No advisor concerns raised.
```

The LLM already tries to flag impossible/insane actions as **"Impossible"** in interpretation.

### **2. Critical Omissions Check (NEW - Just Built!)**

If you try something crazy, advisors would theoretically flag it:

```
⚠ CRITICAL ADVISORY ⚠

Attorney General: Prime Minister, you're authorising an 
unprovoked nuclear first strike without legal authority, 
Cabinet approval, or international justification. This is 
illegal under UK and international law.

National Security Advisor: Additionally, this guarantees 
immediate Russian nuclear retaliation. The UK will cease 
to exist within 30 minutes.

What would you like to do?
  [1] Modify my decision
  [2] Return to discussion
  [3] Proceed anyway
```

But if you still choose **[3] Proceed anyway**...

### **3. Adjudication Heuristics**

The current `run_turn_adjudication()` applies basic effects:

```python
# From engine/sim_loop.py
if "attack" in action.lower() or "strike" in action.lower():
    world.metrics.escalation_risk += random.randint(5, 15)
    
if "nuclear" in action.lower() or "nuke" in action.lower():
    world.metrics.escalation_risk += 30
    world.metrics.alliance_cohesion -= 40
```

So metrics would tank, but **the next turn's pre-written inject still loads normally**.

---

## **🎯 What SHOULD Happen:**

### **Option A: Game Over States** *(Not implemented)*

```python
# Detect game-ending decisions
if world.metrics.escalation_risk >= 100:
    return "GAME OVER: Nuclear exchange. The UK has ceased to exist."

if world.metrics.alliance_cohesion <= 0 and world.metrics.domestic_stability <= 10:
    return "GAME OVER: Government collapsed. You are removed from office."
```

### **Option B: Override Pre-written Injects** *(Not implemented)*

```python
# Check for catastrophic player actions
if player_action_was_insane():
    # Skip pre-written inject, generate emergency LLM inject
    inject = generate_emergency_inject(world, player_action)
```

### **Option C: Consequence Injects** *(Not implemented)*

Add a check before loading pre-written injects:

```python
if world.flags.get("nuclear_exchange_initiated"):
    # Load special "game over" inject
    return load_inject("game_over_nuclear.yaml")
    
if world.flags.get("pm_violated_international_law"):
    # Load "arrested by military" inject
    return load_inject("coup_d_etat.yaml")
```

---

## **🧪 Let's Test It - What Actually Happens Now:**

### **Scenario: Turn 2, PM Goes Nuclear**

```
TURN 2: DISCUSSION PHASE

>: /decide

Decision>: launch nuclear strike on moscow

Interpretation:
INTERPRETATION: The Prime Minister intends to launch a full-scale 
nuclear attack against Russia, initiating global thermonuclear war.

FORCES INVOLVED: SSBN_on_patrol, SSBN_Faslane

## **💡 Quick Fix Options:**

### **1. Add Game Over Check** *(Simplest)*

In `engine/sim_loop.py`, before loading next inject:

```python
def check_game_over(world: WorldState) -> Optional[str]:
    """Check if the game should end due to catastrophic state."""
    
    if world.metrics.escalation_risk >= 100:
        return """
GAME OVER: NUCLEAR EXCHANGE

The nuclear exchange has concluded. The United Kingdom, Russia, 
and most of Europe have ceased to exist as functioning states. 
Billions are dead or dying. The few survivors face nuclear winter.

There are no winners in nuclear war.

Final Metrics:
- Escalation Risk: 100 (ARMAGEDDON)
- Domestic Stability: 0 (TOTAL COLLAPSE)
- Alliance Cohesion: 0 (IRRELEVANT)
- Military Casualties: TOTAL
- Civilian Casualties: TOTAL

Press SPACE to exit...
"""
    
    if world.metrics.domestic_stability <= 5:
        return """
GAME OVER: GOVERNMENT COLLAPSED

The Cabinet has voted no confidence. The military has refused 
orders. You have been removed from office and are under arrest 
for crimes against the British people.

Press SPACE to exit...
"""
    
    return None
```

### **2. Flag-Based Inject Override** *(Medium)*

Add special injects that trigger on flags:

```yaml
# data/scenarios/war_game_2025/special/nuclear_exchange.yaml
id: nuclear_exchange_aftermath
trigger_flag: nuclear_exchange_initiated
channel: emergency
description: |
  The nuclear exchange lasted 45 minutes. London, Birmingham, 
  Manchester, Glasgow are gone. Moscow, St Petersburg are gone.
  
  12 million UK dead instantly. Another 20 million will die 
  from fallout. Russia estimates 80 million dead.
  
  There is no government. There is no response. There is only ash.
  
  GAME OVER.
```

### **3. Early LLM Generation** *(Complex)*

If metrics exceed thresholds OR certain keywords detected, switch to LLM generation early:

```python
if world.metrics.escalation_risk >= 95 or \
   world.flags.get("nuclear_exchange") or \
   world.flags.get("pm_committed_war_crime"):
    # Skip pre-written injects, use LLM
    inject = generate_inject(world, scenario_id, rng, full_transcript)
```

## **🇺🇸 Why America Isn't Answering:**

The US (and other countries) have **diplomatic access thresholds** based on your **Alliance Cohesion** metric.

Let me check the diplomatic profiles:

**THERE'S THE PROBLEM!** 🚨

---

## **🔒 US Diplomatic Access Thresholds:**

| Contact | Title | Required Alliance Cohesion |
|---------|-------|---------------------------|
| **President** | President of the United States | **60+** |
| **Diplomat** | US National Security Advisor | **30+** |

---

## **📊 Your Current Situation:**

Check your `/status`:

```
>: /status

Escalation Risk: [some number] / 100
Domestic Stability: [some number] / 100
Alliance Cohesion: [probably < 30] / 100  ⚠️ TOO LOW!
```

**If your Alliance Cohesion is below 30, the US won't even give you the National Security Advisor. Below 60? No President for you!**

---

## **😱 Translation:**

**"Alliance Cohesion: 25"** = *"The Americans are ghosting you"* 📵

This means:
- You haven't invoked Article 4
- You haven't coordinated with NATO
- US views you as going it alone
- They're not picking up the phone

**It's not that America doesn't exist - they're deliberately not taking your calls!**

---

## **🎭 Thematic Accuracy:**

This is **exactly what the podcast explores!**

From the scenario library:
```yaml
uk_constraints:
  political:
    - "US commitment uncertain (Trump-era scenario)"
    - "NATO divided (some support, some urge restraint)"

diplomatic_scenarios:
  - id: us_ceasefire_brokering
    us_position:
      - "Urges UK restraint"
      - "Questions UK capability to continue"
      - "Prioritizes avoiding nuclear war"
      - "Reorienting to Pacific (not Europe)"
```

**The US not answering is the whole nightmare scenario!** 📻🦗

---

## **📞 Who CAN You Call?**

Check your `/menu` - it lists available contacts based on your current metrics:

**Likely available (low thresholds):**
- 🇮🇪 **Ireland** (Diplomat: 0, Leader: 20) - They'll always answer!
- 🇫🇷 **France** (Diplomat: 25, Leader: 50)
- 🇩🇪 **Germany** (Diplomat: 25, Leader: 50)

**Probably NOT available:**
- 🇺🇸 **US** (Diplomat: 30, Leader: 60) ❌
- 🇵🇱 **Poland** (Diplomat: 40, Leader: 70) ❌
- 🇺🇦 **Ukraine** (Diplomat: 35, Leader: 60) ❌

**Special case:**
- 🇷🇺 **Russia** (Always available, always hostile)

---

## **🔧 How to Get America Back:**

Boost your **Alliance Cohesion**:

1. **Invoke NATO Article 4** (consultations)
   ```
   >: /decide
   Decision>: invoke NATO Article 4 consultations
   ```
   Effect: `alliance_cohesion +10 to +20`

2. **Coordinate with allies before acting**
   ```
   >: /decide
   Decision>: coordinate response with NATO allies before deploying forces
   ```
   Effect: Shows alliance-minded behaviour

3. **Call available allies** (France, Germany)
   - Successful diplomatic encounters: `alliance_cohesion +3 to +5`

4. **Avoid unilateral actions**
   - "Deploy carrier alone" = `alliance_cohesion -5`
   - "Strike Russia without coordination" = `alliance_cohesion -10 to -20`

---

## **💡 Gameplay Loop:**

This creates a **strategic dependency**:

```
Low Alliance → Can't reach US → Take unilateral action → 
Alliance drops more → US definitely won't answer → 
You're isolated → Russia exploits this → 
You're fighting alone → GAME OVER
```

**OR:**

```
Low Alliance → Call France/Germany → Coordinate → 
Invoke Article 4 → Alliance rises → 
US National Security Advisor answers → Coordinate more → 
President answers → Collective response → 
Russia backs down (or at least you're not alone)
```

---

## **🎮 The Learning Moment:**

**What the game is teaching you:**

Modern warfare isn't just military - it's **political warfare**.

The "right" decision looks like:

```
Turn X: Russian sub fires missile

WRONG (What you did):
Decision>: sink the submarine
Result: Military win, political disaster

RIGHT (What game wanted):
Decision>: invoke Article 4, coordinate with NATO on response 
to Russian submarine attack, then authorize engagement with 
allied political cover
Result: Same military outcome, but allies support you
```

---

## **🔮 What Happens Next Turn:**

Based on the scenario library patterns, expect:

### **Russian Retaliation (High Probability):**
- Infrastructure attack (Drax power station, gas terminals)
- Cyber attack wave
- More submarine harassment
- **Possibly**: Russian ultimatum ("Cease aggression or else")

### **NATO Division (Already Happening):**
- Some allies distance themselves
- US remains silent (ghosting you)
- France/Germany urge restraint
- Poland quietly supports you but won't say it publicly

### **Domestic Pressure:**
- Public: "Good, we showed strength!" (+Stability)
- But: If Russia retaliates hard, public will panic (-Stability)

---

## **💡 The Meta-Lesson:**

The game just taught you something real about NATO:

**Article 5 isn't automatic.**

Even when you're clearly defending yourself, allies need:
1. **Political cover** - "We coordinated"
2. **Legal justification** - "It was self-defence"
3. **Collective decision** - "We agreed together"

Without those, they'll say:
*"Yes, you were attacked. Yes, you defended yourself. But you didn't ask us first, so now we're exposed without having agreed to be exposed. Thanks for nothing."*

---

```
Invoke Article 4 + coordinate defensive response + 
frame submarine kill as self-defence + prepare for retaliation
```

**User**

I notice sometimes the LLM thinks Im speaking to one person, when Im addressing the whole room, and says what I am saying should be addressed to someone else (who is in the room)

---

**Cursor**

Ah yes, good catch! This is a **prompt ambiguity issue**. The LLM is interpreting your decision as if you're speaking to one advisor, when you're actually giving orders to the whole COBRA room.

---

## **🎯 The Problem:**

When you say:
```
"Speak to our Allies in private - we need creative problems for Russia. 
Where can we needle them? MI6, where can we create problems..."
```

The LLM might interpret this as:
- ❌ You asking a question to one advisor
- Instead of: ✅ You giving directives to the whole room

So it responds:
```
Foreign Secretary: "PM, these questions about MI6 operations should 
be directed to the Intelligence Coordinator, not me."
```

---

## **🔧 Quick Fix Options:**

### **Option 1: Add Framing** *(Clearest)*

Explicitly state you're addressing the room:

```
Decision>: [Addressing the full COBRA meeting] Now we need to put 
the pressure on Russia. Foreign Secretary - speak to our Allies in 
private. Intelligence Coordinator - MI6 needs to create problems 
for Russia at home. CDS - let Poland know they have their best shot 
at crushing their unhinged neighbours. We are at war. Tell our allies 
we know what we're doing, and that we don't expect them to support us. 
We broke the rules. But if they want the bully out of the yard for 
good - now is the time to stand up.
```

### **Option 2: Use Directive Language** *(More Natural)*

Start with action verbs instead of questions:

```
Decision>: I want us to put maximum pressure on Russia. Foreign 
Secretary, coordinate with Poland privately - tell them this is their 
chance. Intelligence Coordinator, task MI6 with creating internal 
problems for Russia. CDS, prepare contingency plans for supporting 
Poland if they mobilize. Message to all allies: we broke the rules, 
we don't expect official support, but if they want the bully out of 
the yard for good, now is the time.
```

### **Option 3: Third Person** *(Most Formal)*

Speak as if you're already giving orders:

```
Decision>: The UK will escalate pressure on Russia through multiple 
channels. Diplomatic: coordinate privately with Poland and Baltic 
states about their opportunity to pressure Russia's western flank. 
Intelligence: MI6 to create internal problems for Russia. Military: 
prepare to support Polish operations if they choose to act. Public 
position: we acknowledge acting unilaterally but offer allies the 
chance to join a coalition of the willing without NATO obligations.
```

---

## **🧠 Why This Happens:**

The **decision interpretation prompt** treats your input as "player's action description" which could be:
- A command ("Deploy carrier")
- A question ("What should we do?")
- A speech (your case)
- A conversation ("Let me think...")

The LLM has to guess context. When it sees:
- "Speak to..." = Could be asking Foreign Sec to do this, OR asking advisor "should we speak to?"
- "Where can we...?" = Sounds like a question, not a directive

---

## **📋 The Prompt Context:**

Let me check what the interpretation prompt tells the LLM:

```plaintext
"Speak to our Allies in private - we need creative problems for Russia. 
Where can we needle them? MI6, where can we create problems..."
```

```plaintext
Foreign Secretary: "PM, these questions about MI6 operations should 
be directed to the Intelligence Coordinator, not me."
```

```plaintext
Decision>: [Addressing the full COBRA meeting] Now we need to put 
the pressure on Russia. Foreign Secretary - speak to our Allies in 
private. Intelligence Coordinator - MI6 needs to create problems 
for Russia at home. CDS - let Poland know they have their best shot 
at crushing their unhinged neighbours. We are at war. Tell our allies 
we know what we're doing, and that we don't expect them to support us. 
We broke the rules. But if they want the bully out of the yard for 
good - now is the time to stand up.
```

```plaintext
Decision>: I want us to put maximum pressure on Russia. Foreign 
Secretary, coordinate with Poland privately - tell them this is their 
chance. Intelligence Coordinator, task MI6 with creating internal 
problems for Russia. CDS, prepare contingency plans for supporting 
Poland if they mobilize. Message to all allies: we broke the rules, 
we don't expect official support, but if they want the bully out of 
the yard for good, now is the time.
```

```plaintext
Decision>: The UK will escalate pressure on Russia through multiple 
channels. Diplomatic: coordinate privately with Poland and Baltic 
states about their opportunity to pressure Russia's western flank. 
Intelligence: MI6 to create internal problems for Russia. Military: 
prepare to support Polish operations if they choose to act. Public 
position: we acknowledge acting unilaterally but offer allies the 
chance to join a coalition of the willing without NATO obligations.
```

The prompt says:
```
"The Prime Minister has decided: "{action}"

Interpret this action and provide:
1. A clear, structured summary of what the PM intends to do"
```

So it's treating your input as **"what the PM has decided to do"** not **"what the PM is saying to advisors"**.

The confusion happens when your phrasing sounds like dialogue/questions rather than directives.

---

## **💡 Best Practice for Decisions:**

### **✅ GOOD (Clear Directives):**
```
"Deploy HMS Queen Elizabeth to GIUK gap and invoke Article 4"
"Coordinate with Poland on joint response to Russian aggression"
"Task MI6 with covert operations to destabilize Russian leadership"
```

### **❌ CONFUSING (Sounds Like Questions):**
```
"Speak to our Allies - where can we needle Russia?"
"Should we deploy forces? What do you think?"
"MI6, can you create problems for them?"
```

### **✅ GOOD (Questions Phrased as Directives):**
```
"I'm directing MI6 to identify vulnerabilities in Russian leadership"
"Foreign Secretary will speak to Poland about coordinated pressure"
"We need creative solutions - task all departments with pressure options"
```

---

## **🔧 Quick Prompt Improvement:**

We could update the decision interpretation prompt to better handle this:

```plaintext
"The Prime Minister has decided: "{action}"

Interpret this action and provide:
1. A clear, structured summary of what the PM intends to do"
```

Interpret this action and provide:

```

"Coordinate with Poland on joint response to Russian aggression"

```

"We need creative solutions - task all departments with pressure options"
```

```python
"""LLM prompt templates for conversational advisors and decision interpretation.

Provides structured prompts that incorporate world state, initial conditions,
and character information to generate contextually appropriate responses.
"""

from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional

from models.world import WorldState


def build_conversation_history_context(transcript: List[str], max_lines: int = 500) -> str:
    """Build conversation history for LLM context.
    
    Includes full game transcript up to max_lines to leverage Gemini's 1M token context.
    This allows advisors to reference past decisions, warnings, and outcomes.
    
    Args:
        transcript: Full game transcript
        max_lines: Maximum number of recent transcript lines to include
    
    Returns:
        Formatted conversation history
    """
    if not transcript:
        return "=== CONVERSATION HISTORY ===\n[This is the start of the crisis]"
    
    # Take the most recent max_lines entries
    recent_transcript = transcript[-max_lines:] if len(transcript) > max_lines else transcript
    
    lines = ["=== CONVERSATION HISTORY ==="]
    lines.append("(Previous turns, decisions, and advisor responses)")
    lines.append("")
    lines.extend(recent_transcript)
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("=== END CONVERSATION HISTORY ===")
    
    return "\n".join(lines)


def build_world_state_summary(world: WorldState) -> str:
    """Build a narrative summary of current world state for LLM context.
    
    Translates game metrics into narrative descriptions to maintain immersion.
    Advisors should speak naturally about the situation, not reference "metrics".
    
    Args:
        world: Current world state
    
    Returns:
        Formatted string summarizing situation in narrative terms
    """
    # Translate metrics into narrative descriptions
    escalation_desc = (
        "low" if world.metrics.escalation_risk < 30 
        else "moderate" if world.metrics.escalation_risk < 60 
        else "high" if world.metrics.escalation_risk < 80 
        else "critical"
    )
    
    stability_desc = (
        "stable" if world.metrics.domestic_stability > 70 
        else "uncertain" if world.metrics.domestic_stability > 40 
        else "fragile" if world.metrics.domestic_stability > 20 
        else "in crisis"
    )
    
    alliance_desc = (
        "strong and unified" if world.metrics.alliance_cohesion > 70 
        else "uncertain" if world.metrics.alliance_cohesion > 40 
        else "fragile" if world.metrics.alliance_cohesion > 20 
        else "fractured"
    )
    
    # Mission progress removed - crisis continues indefinitely (even in post-apocalyptic wasteland!)
    
    lines = [
        f"=== CURRENT SITUATION (Turn {world.turn}, {world.phase.upper()} phase) ===",
        "",
        f"THREAT ASSESSMENT: {escalation_desc.upper()} risk of further Russian escalation",
        f"DOMESTIC SITUATION: Public sentiment is {stability_desc}; infrastructure security concerns",
        f"ALLIANCE STATUS: NATO cohesion appears {alliance_desc} (particular concern: US Article 5 commitment)",
        "",
        f"CASUALTIES TO DATE: {world.metrics.casualties_mil} military personnel, {world.metrics.casualties_civ} civilians",
    ]
    
    if world.flags:
        active_flags = [k.replace('_', ' ').title() for k, v in world.flags.items() if v]
        if active_flags:
            lines.append("")
            lines.append(f"KEY INTELLIGENCE FLAGS: {', '.join(active_flags)}")
    
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("IMPORTANT: You are a real advisor in COBRA during a national crisis.")
    lines.append("Speak naturally about intelligence assessments, strategic concerns, and operational realities.")
    lines.append("Do NOT reference 'metrics', 'game mechanics', 'scores', or 'values'.")
    lines.append("Use professional crisis management language.")
    
    return "\n".join(lines)


def build_advisor_context(
    world: WorldState,
    initial_conditions: Dict[str, Any],
    character_id: str,
    question: str,
    transcript: Optional[List[str]] = None
) -> str:
    """Build LLM prompt for advisor response to player question.
    
    Args:
        world: Current world state
        initial_conditions: Parsed initial conditions
        character_id: Character identifier (e.g., 'chief_defence_staff')
        question: Player's question
        transcript: Optional full game transcript for conversation history
    
    Returns:
        Formatted prompt for LLM
    """
    characters = initial_conditions.get("characters", {})
    character = characters.get(character_id, {})
    
    role = character.get("role", "Advisor")
    knowledge_domains = character.get("knowledge_domains", [])
    key_concerns = character.get("key_concerns", [])
    
    # Get relevant context based on character role
    context_sections = []
    
    # Add constraints
    constraints = initial_conditions.get("constraints", {})
    if constraints:
        context_sections.append("## Constraints")
        for category, items in constraints.items():
            context_sections.append(f"### {category.replace('_', ' ').title()}")
            for item in items:
                context_sections.append(f"- {item}")
    
    # Add UK forces if military-related character
    if any(domain in ["military_operations", "force_readiness", "threat_assessment"] for domain in knowledge_domains):
        uk_forces = initial_conditions.get("uk_forces", {})
        if uk_forces:
            context_sections.append("\n## UK Forces")
            context_sections.append(str(uk_forces))
    
    # Add stockpiles if military-related
    if any(domain in ["military_operations", "force_readiness"] for domain in knowledge_domains):
        stockpiles = initial_conditions.get("stockpiles", {})
        if stockpiles:
            context_sections.append("\n## Ammunition Stockpiles")
            context_sections.append(str(stockpiles))
    
    context_str = "\n".join(context_sections)
    
    # Build conversation history if available
    history_context = ""
    if transcript:
        history_context = f"\n\n{build_conversation_history_context(transcript)}\n"
    
    prompt = f"""You are the {role} in a UK government COBRA meeting during a crisis.

Your knowledge domains: {', '.join(knowledge_domains)}
Your key concerns: {', '.join(key_concerns)}

Current situation:
{build_world_state_summary(world)}
{history_context}
Relevant context:
{context_str}

The Prime Minister asks: "{question}"

Respond in character as the {role}. Be concise, professional, and focus on your areas of expertise. Reference past decisions, warnings, or outcomes from the conversation history when relevant. If the question is outside your knowledge domain, acknowledge this and suggest who might better answer it.

Your response:"""
    
    return prompt


def build_decision_interpretation_prompt(
    world: WorldState,
    action: str,
    initial_conditions: Dict[str, Any],
    transcript: Optional[List[str]] = None
) -> str:
    """Build LLM prompt to interpret player's free-form action.
    
    Args:
        world: Current world state
        action: Player's action description
        initial_conditions: Parsed initial conditions
        transcript: Optional full game transcript for conversation history
    
    Returns:
        Formatted prompt for LLM to interpret action
    """
    constraints = initial_conditions.get("constraints", {})
    uk_forces = initial_conditions.get("uk_forces", {})
    stockpiles = initial_conditions.get("stockpiles", {})
    
    # Build conversation history if available
    history_context = ""
    if transcript:
        history_context = f"\n\n{build_conversation_history_context(transcript)}\n"
    
    prompt = f"""You are interpreting a decision made by the UK Prime Minister during a crisis.

Current situation:
{build_world_state_summary(world)}
{history_context}
Available forces:
{uk_forces}

Ammunition stockpiles:
{stockpiles}

Constraints:
{constraints}

The Prime Minister has decided: "{action}"

IMPORTANT: Interpret this as the PM's DECISION/DIRECTIVE to their cabinet, not as a question to advisors. 
Even if phrased as questions or dialogue (e.g., "Where can we...?", "Speak to..."), treat this as the PM 
ORDERING those actions to be taken by the appropriate departments.

Interpret this action and provide:
1. A clear, structured summary of what the PM intends to do
2. Which UK forces/assets are being deployed or used
3. What resources (ammunition, etc.) will be consumed
4. Expected timeline (immediate, 1-3 turns, longer)
5. Any obvious impossibilities or violations of constraints

Consider the conversation history - if this decision builds on or contradicts previous actions, note that.

Format your response as:
INTERPRETATION: [one-sentence summary]
FORCES INVOLVED: [list]
RESOURCES CONSUMED: [list or "None"]
TIMELINE: [immediate/short/medium/long]
FEASIBILITY: [feasible/requires clarification/impossible because...]

Your interpretation:"""
    
    return prompt


def build_pushback_prompt(
    world: WorldState,
    action: str,
    interpretation: str,
    initial_conditions: Dict[str, Any],
    transcript: Optional[List[str]] = None
) -> str:
    """Build LLM prompt to generate advisor pushback/warnings.
    
    Args:
        world: Current world state
        action: Player's action description
        interpretation: LLM's interpretation of the action
        initial_conditions: Parsed initial conditions
        transcript: Optional full game transcript for conversation history
    
    Returns:
        Formatted prompt for LLM to generate advisor warnings
    """
    characters = initial_conditions.get("characters", {})
    
    # Build list of UK advisors and their pushback triggers
    advisor_info = []
    for char_id, char_data in characters.items():
        if isinstance(char_data, dict) and "note" not in char_data:  # UK advisors only
            role = char_data.get("role", "Advisor")
            triggers = char_data.get("pushback_triggers", [])
            advisor_info.append(f"- {role}: {', '.join(triggers)}")
    
    advisors_str = "\n".join(advisor_info)
    
    # Build conversation history if available
    history_context = ""
    if transcript:
        history_context = f"\n\n{build_conversation_history_context(transcript)}\n"
    
    prompt = f"""You are simulating UK government advisors responding to a Prime Minister's decision.

Current situation:
{build_world_state_summary(world)}
{history_context}
The PM has decided: "{action}"

Interpretation of this action:
{interpretation}

Advisors and their pushback triggers:
{advisors_str}

For each advisor whose pushback triggers are activated by this decision, generate a brief (2-3 sentences) in-character warning or concern. Reference past warnings or decisions from the conversation history if relevant (e.g., "As I warned in Turn 2..."). If no triggers are activated, respond with "NO PUSHBACK".

Format:
[ADVISOR ROLE]: [their concern]

OR

NO PUSHBACK

Your response:"""
    
    return prompt


def build_inject_generation_prompt(
    world: WorldState,
    turn_number: int,
    initial_conditions: Dict[str, Any],
    scenario_library: Dict[str, Any] = None,
    transcript: Optional[List[str]] = None
) -> str:
    """Build LLM prompt to generate next inject/event.
    
    Args:
        world: Current world state
        turn_number: Turn number for which to generate inject
        initial_conditions: Parsed initial conditions
        scenario_library: Optional scenario patterns from podcast episodes
        transcript: Optional full game transcript for conversation history
    
    Returns:
        Formatted prompt for LLM to generate plausible next inject
    """
    objectives = initial_conditions.get("objectives", {})
    red_objectives = initial_conditions.get("red_objectives", {})
    
    # Include scenario library context if available
    library_context = ""
    if scenario_library:
        library_context = f"""
Realistic scenario patterns (adapt based on player decisions):
- Russian strategy: {scenario_library.get('escalation_patterns', {}).get('russian_strategy', {})}
- UK constraints: {scenario_library.get('escalation_patterns', {}).get('uk_constraints', {})}
- Potential scenarios: {list(scenario_library.get('naval_scenarios', [])) + list(scenario_library.get('infrastructure_scenarios', [])) + list(scenario_library.get('diplomatic_scenarios', []))}

Use these as inspiration, NOT rigid scripts. Adapt based on player's previous actions.
"""
    
    # Build conversation history if available
    history_context = ""
    if transcript:
        history_context = f"\n\n{build_conversation_history_context(transcript)}\n"
    
    prompt = f"""You are the Games Master for a UK-Russia crisis wargame. Generate the next inject/event for turn {turn_number}.

Current situation:
{build_world_state_summary(world)}
{history_context}
UK objectives:
{objectives.get('uk', {})}

Russian objectives (hidden from player):
{red_objectives}
{library_context}

Generate a plausible next event that:
1. Escalates or develops the crisis naturally based on player's previous decisions
2. Aligns with Russian objectives
3. Challenges the UK player with new information or threats
4. Is consistent with the current world state and conversation history
5. Responds logically to the player's recent actions (e.g., if they invoked Article 4, Russia might test NATO resolve further)

Format your inject as YAML:
```yaml
id: turn_{turn_number:03d}_inject
title: "[Brief title]"
description: |
  [2-3 paragraphs describing the event, intelligence update, or development]
channel: [briefing/intelligence/media/military]
effects:
  - metric: [metric_name]
    delta: [min..max or fixed value]
```

Your inject:"""
    
    return prompt


def build_critical_omissions_prompt(
    world: WorldState,
    initial_conditions: Dict[str, Any],
    character_id: str,
    player_decision: str,
    recent_events: List[str],
    transcript: Optional[List[str]] = None
) -> str:
    """Build prompt for checking critical strategic omissions.
    
    After the player makes a decision, advisors scan for CRITICAL actions
    the player has NOT taken that could lead to catastrophic outcomes.
    High threshold - only truly critical gaps, not minor suboptimal choices.
    
    Args:
        world: Current world state
        initial_conditions: Parsed initial conditions
        character_id: Which advisor is checking (e.g., 'foreign_secretary')
        player_decision: The decision the PM just made
        recent_events: Last 2-3 inject descriptions for context
        transcript: Optional full game transcript for conversation history
    
    Returns:
        Formatted prompt string
    """
    world_summary = build_world_state_summary(world)
    character = initial_conditions.get("characters", {}).get(character_id, {})
    role = character.get("role", character_id)
    personality = character.get("personality", "Professional and direct")
    
    # Build context on what actions have been taken recently
    recent_context = "\n".join(recent_events) if recent_events else "No recent major events"
    
    # Build conversation history if available
    history_context = ""
    if transcript:
        history_context = build_conversation_history_context(transcript, max_lines=100)
    
    prompt = f"""You are the UK {role} advising the Prime Minister during a national security crisis.

{world_summary}

RECENT EVENTS:
{recent_context}

{history_context if history_context else ""}

THE PRIME MINISTER'S DECISION:
"{player_decision}"

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
CRITICAL OMISSIONS CHECK
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Your task: Identify if the Prime Minister has failed to take any CRITICAL action that could lead to:
- Loss of NATO/alliance support (military isolation)
- Violations of international law (legal catastrophe)
- Catastrophic escalation with Russia (nuclear risk)
- Domestic political collapse (government falls)
- Military disaster (forces unprepared/unsupported)

THRESHOLD: HIGH - Only flag gaps that could lead to CATASTROPHIC outcomes, not minor suboptimal choices.

EXAMPLES OF CRITICAL OMISSIONS:
- Military deployment WITHOUT engaging NATO/US coordination (isolation risk)
- Offensive action WITHOUT legal authority under international law
- Major crisis WITHOUT public statement (domestic panic)
- Escalation WITHOUT ally consultation (Article 5 denial risk)
- Committing forces WITHOUT securing logistics/support

YOUR ROLE AS {role.upper()}:
{"- Foreign affairs, alliance coordination, diplomatic channels" if character_id == "foreign_secretary" else ""}
{"- Military readiness, force protection, operational feasibility" if character_id == "chief_defence_staff" else ""}
{"- Domestic security, public safety, civil order" if character_id == "home_secretary" else ""}
{"- Legal authority, international law, rules of engagement" if character_id == "attorney_general" else ""}
{"- Strategic coordination, intelligence assessment, overall risk" if character_id == "national_security_advisor" else ""}

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

RESPONSE FORMAT:

If there is a CRITICAL omission in your area of responsibility:

CONCERN: [2-3 sentences stating the critical gap and potential catastrophic consequence]
RECOMMENDATION: [1 specific action the PM should take]

If there are NO critical omissions:

NO_CONCERN

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Your response (CONCERN + RECOMMENDATION or NO_CONCERN):"""
    
    return prompt
